text:
Title: Investigating Curriculum Alignment with 21st Century Skills for Graduate Employability: The Case of a Professional Bachelor’s Program in a Moroccan University Purpose: This paper examines the alignment between the curriculum of a professional Bachelor’s degree program at a Moroccan university and 21st century skills required for graduate employability in the 21st century. Methodology: Mixed-methods research approach was employed in this study. It started by carrying out a comprehensive curriculum review exercise, in addition to administering a questionnaire to 89 students and conducting semi-structured interviews with 9 faculty members. The curriculum review focused on the presence of key competencies such as critical thinking, creativity and digital skills. In addition, the students’ perception about their readiness to enter the labour market was measured quantitatively through the questionnaire, while the faculty members’ views about the effectiveness of their pedagogical practices were examined qualitatively.

These results highlight a considerable gap between intended and actual learning outcomes. While the curriculum analysis and students’ comments revealed that the content taught is outdated and lacks any relation to the 21st century skills, faculty members contended that students are adequately prepared and that any inadequacies in this regard could be attributed to poor attendance rates. However, a large majority of students believed that they were not equipped to face the realities of the job market, a phenomenon attributed to the theoretical nature of the content imparted and lack of exposure to the practical aspects. The study concludes by recommending an urgent overhaul of the curriculum in line with the emerging requirements and needs of the job market. It also advocates replacing the conventional teaching methodology with a more participatory and project-based learning approach, while developing students’ metacognitive skills to foster their capacity for metacognitive resilience and self-motivation to learn, thereby helping them cope with the changing demands of a rapidly changing global economy.
explanation: The text is a well-structured academic abstract with plausible methodology and locale details; however, it also uses several buzzwords and repetitive phrases typical of AI-written summaries, making it ambiguous.
